items.title: Kyrgyz Cyrillic adds a few letters beyond Russian
items.summary: You do not need the whole alphabet at once. Start by noticing the extra learner-critical letters like `ө`, `ү`, and `ң`, because they carry real meaning differences.

items.title: Front rounded vowels form a family
items.summary: The letters `ө` and `ү` belong to a front-vowel family. Later they often help predict which suffix shape sounds natural, so it is worth recognizing them early by sight and sound.

items.title: `ң` writes one sound, not two
items.summary: When you see `ң`, pronounce it like the `ng` in `sing`. Treat it as one sound chunk rather than `n` plus `g`.
